changedFiles.reason: Declared fail-closed, Coverage, readiness, and core-test scope.
Recorded implementation evidence and remaining release/test risks.
Defined adoption readiness as a static completeness gate with independent CI requirements.
Added root and nested cross-stack production/test path heuristics.
Made all missing project quality defaults fail closed.
Raised the repository script coverage floor from 55 to 60 percent.
Narrowed semantics to static completeness and rejected exact trivial no-op commands.
Verified missing stack files make every project quality target and quality fail.
Covered root Go/C#/TypeScript and monorepo/Maven/Flutter Coverage layouts.
Covered static readiness output and trivial command rejection.
Added direct tests for Review Policy, Status Check, and Status Consistency.
Documented static readiness limits and the 60 percent coverage floor.
Applied equivalent Japanese readiness and coverage wording.
Applied equivalent Chinese readiness and coverage wording.
Clarified static completeness versus command effectiveness.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Missing configuration must fail closed with actionable errors.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Missing Makefile.ai.stack causes all project targets and make quality to emit ERROR and exit nonzero.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Coverage defaults are reviewable heuristics, not universal framework guarantees.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Policy adds common root/nested layouts while adoptionReviewed and documentation retain mandatory project review.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Static configuration checks must not overclaim runtime or test effectiveness.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: CLI and multilingual docs explicitly require independent make quality and check-ai-pr results.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Compatibility-preserving command names should be retained when documentation can accurately narrow semantics.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: check-ai-adoption-ready remains available but consistently identifies itself as static configuration completeness.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not hand-edit generated cockpit status.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Status was generated by lifecycle commands.

risk.detail: Installed fail-closed behavior and default Coverage matching affect every adopted repository.

observedIssues: Missing Makefile.ai.stack previously allowed make quality to succeed with informational output.
Python fnmatch does not make **/*.ext match root files, requiring explicit root patterns.
Static command text cannot establish that arbitrary project checks are semantically effective.

residualRisks.detail: No new public tag, checksum archive, signature, or provenance was published.
Default paths remain heuristics; custom monorepos, generated code, and framework layouts require project review.
Status Check reached 73%, but Review Policy is 44%, Status Consistency 48%, Scope 33%, Checkpoint 24%, Finish 27%, and Status Generation 35%.

reviewReadiness.reason: Fail-open defaults, reported root Coverage gaps, readiness overclaim, and two zero-coverage core modules are addressed; publication and broader core coverage remain.

checkpointEvidence.detail: Fail-open reproduction, fnmatch root behavior, readiness semantics, and core coverage baseline reviewed.
105 tests, 62.89 percent coverage, fail-closed Make, Coverage layouts, static readiness, and release contract reviewed.

knownGaps: A new public release and integrity artifacts have not been published.
Several lifecycle-critical modules remain below 70 percent direct coverage.
Minimal/core/full installation modes are not implemented.
overclaimPrevention: Describe Coverage paths as heuristics and adoption readiness as static completeness; require independent project quality, PR, and release evidence.
